title: Quiz: Noise Sources in Quantum Hardware
description: Test your understanding of quantum noise physics, classification, and the relationship between noise spectra and decoherence

questions.question: Which type of noise primarily contributes to T1 (energy relaxation) processes?

options.text: Low-frequency noise that commutes with the qubit Hamiltonian
Transverse noise at the qubit transition frequency
Static offset noise at zero frequency
High-frequency noise well above the qubit frequency

questions.explanation: T1 relaxation is caused by transverse noise (noise that does not commute with the qubit Hamiltonian) at the qubit transition frequency. This noise can induce transitions between |0> and |1> states. The rate is given by Gamma_1 = (1/2) S_perp(omega_01), where S_perp is the transverse noise power spectral density evaluated at the qubit frequency.

questions.question: In the spin-boson model, an Ohmic bath has spectral density J(omega) proportional to:

options.text: omega^0 (constant)
omega^1 (linear)
omega^2 (quadratic)
1/omega (inverse)

questions.explanation: An Ohmic bath has J(omega) proportional to omega at low frequencies. This is characteristic of resistive environments. Sub-ohmic baths have J ~ omega^s with s < 1, while super-ohmic baths have s > 1. The ohmicity parameter s determines the nature of decoherence dynamics.

questions.question: What is the relationship between T1, T2, and pure dephasing time T_phi?

options.text: T2 = T1 + T_phi
T2 = min(T1, T_phi)

questions.explanation: The total dephasing rate is 1/T2 = 1/(2*T1) + 1/T_phi. The factor of 1/2 appears because T1 processes randomize both amplitude and phase, while only half of that contributes to phase randomization. Pure dephasing (T_phi) adds to this to give the total T2.

questions.question: Markovian noise always leads to exponential decay of coherence, while non-Markovian noise can lead to non-exponential decay patterns.

options.text: True
False

questions.explanation: True! Markovian noise has no memory effects, leading to a constant decay rate and thus exponential coherence decay. Non-Markovian noise has memory, meaning the bath can retain and feed back information to the qubit. This leads to non-exponential decay (often Gaussian at short times) and can even cause temporary coherence recovery (information backflow).

questions.question: Why does the Hahn echo sequence suppress low-frequency noise compared to free induction decay (Ramsey)?

options.text: The echo pulse adds energy that cancels the noise
The echo filter function has a zero at omega = 0, rejecting DC and low-frequency noise
The echo sequence is shorter, giving noise less time to act
The echo pulse removes the qubit from its environment temporarily

questions.explanation: The Hahn echo filter function |F(omega)|^2 = 16*sin^4(omega*t/4) has a zero at omega = 0, meaning it rejects DC and low-frequency noise components. The pi pulse in the middle of the sequence reverses the phase accumulation from slow noise, effectively canceling its effect. This is why T2 (echo) is typically longer than T2* (Ramsey).

questions.question: Which of the following are characteristic signatures of non-Markovian noise? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Gaussian decay at short times rather than exponential
1/f or other colored noise spectrum
Possible temporary increase in coherence (information backflow)
White noise spectrum at all frequencies

questions.explanation: Non-Markovian noise signatures include: (a) Gaussian decay at short times because the noise hasn't had time to become random, (b) 1/f or colored noise spectra indicating frequency-dependent correlations, and (c) possible information backflow where coherence temporarily increases. White noise (d) is characteristic of Markovian, memoryless processes.

questions.question: For a superconducting transmon qubit, which noise source is primarily responsible for charge noise sensitivity?

options.text: Thermal photons in the resonator
Two-level system defects at material interfaces
Cosmic ray impacts
Johnson noise from the control electronics

questions.explanation: Two-level system (TLS) defects at material interfaces (substrate-air, substrate-metal, metal-air) are the primary source of charge noise in superconducting qubits. These defects have fluctuating electric dipole moments that couple capacitively to the qubit, causing both 1/f charge noise and individual telegraph noise events.

questions.question: The filter function formalism relates noise to decoherence through which quantity?

options.text: The product of noise amplitude and measurement time
The integral of noise PSD weighted by the filter function squared divided by omega squared
The sum of all noise frequencies present
The ratio of qubit frequency to noise frequency

questions.explanation: The filter function formalism gives the phase variance as: <phi^2(t)> = integral of [S(omega) * |F(omega,t)|^2 / omega^2] d(omega)/(2*pi). This shows how different control sequences (encoded in F) filter the noise spectrum S(omega) differently. The 1/omega^2 factor arises from integrating frequency noise to get phase.
